fields.title: Environmental Sciences
fields.description: Research focused on climate change, environmental monitoring, water quality, and ecosystem sustainability in Nepal and the Himalayan region.

fields.title: Renewable Energy
fields.description: Exploring solar, hydropower, wind, and biomass energy solutions suitable for Nepal's geography and energy needs.

fields.title: Applied Technology
fields.description: Practical application of emerging technologies including IoT, AI, and data science to solve real-world problems.

fields.title: Social Development
fields.description: Community-based research addressing poverty, education, gender equity, and rural development across Nepal.

fields.title: Civil & Infrastructure
fields.description: Structural engineering, disaster resilience, earthquake engineering, and sustainable construction research.

fields.title: Health Sciences
fields.description: Public health, epidemiology, and health technology research aimed at improving community healthcare outcomes.

fields.name: Dr. Ram Prasad Sharma
fields.bio: PhD in Applied Sciences with over 15 years of experience in research and academic leadership. Specialises in environmental sustainability and climate change.

fields.name: Dr. Sita Devi Poudel
fields.bio: Expert in solar and hydropower technologies with published research in international journals.

fields.name: Er. Bikash Thapa
fields.bio: Specialises in IoT applications and data-driven solutions for engineering problems in Nepal.

fields.name: Ms. Anita Karki
fields.bio: Focuses on community-based participatory research and gender equity programmes.

fields.title: Assessment of Solar Energy Potential in Bagmati Province, Nepal
fields.authors: Poudel S.D., Sharma R.P., Thapa B.
fields.abstract: This study assesses the solar energy potential across different districts of Bagmati Province using GIS-based analysis and ground-truth measurements.

fields.title: Climate Change Impacts on Himalayan Watersheds: A Case Study
fields.authors: Sharma R.P., Karki A., Shrestha K.
fields.abstract: Analysis of long-term precipitation and temperature trends and their effects on high-altitude water resources in central Nepal.

fields.title: IoT-based Structural Health Monitoring for Earthquake-Prone Buildings
fields.journal_or_venue: International Conference on Contemporary Issues of Climate Change (ICICС 2023)
fields.abstract: Prototype IoT sensor network for real-time monitoring of structural integrity in reinforced concrete buildings.

fields.title: Gender Equity in STEM Education: Challenges and Opportunities in Nepal
fields.abstract: A mixed-methods study exploring barriers and enablers of women's participation in science and technology programmes in Nepalese universities.

fields.title: Solar Microgrids for Rural Electrification in Remote Nepal
fields.description: This project aims to design, deploy, and evaluate solar microgrid systems in 10 remote villages of Dolpa and Humla districts where grid connectivity is infeasible. The initiative combines technical engineering work with community engagement to ensure long-term sustainability.

fields.title: Urban Air Quality Monitoring – Kathmandu Valley
fields.description: Deployment of a network of low-cost air quality sensors across Kathmandu Valley to generate real-time pollution data, identify hotspots, and inform policy-level interventions.

fields.title: Women in STEM: Scholarship and Mentorship Programme
fields.description: A two-year initiative providing scholarships, mentorship, and career support to female undergraduate students in engineering and applied sciences across Nepal.

fields.title: International Conference on Contemporary Issues of Climate Change
fields.description: A two-day international conference bringing together researchers, policymakers, and practitioners to discuss climate change impacts, adaptation strategies, and mitigation measures relevant to South Asia.

fields.title: Workshop on Research Methodology for Postgraduates
fields.description: A hands-on two-day workshop covering quantitative and qualitative research methods, statistical analysis using SPSS/R, and academic writing skills for graduate students.

fields.title: Webinar: AI Applications in Environmental Monitoring
fields.description: An online session exploring how artificial intelligence and machine learning are transforming environmental monitoring and data analysis in developing countries.

fields.title: ICAS Receives IDRC Grant for Urban Air Quality Research
fields.content: We are pleased to announce that ICAS has been awarded a research grant of NPR 28,00,000 from the International Development Research Centre (IDRC) to conduct a comprehensive urban air quality monitoring study across the Kathmandu Valley. The two-year project will deploy a network of sensors and generate actionable data for policymakers.

fields.title: ICAS Hosts Successful International Climate Conference
fields.content: The International Conference on Contemporary Issues of Climate Change, organised by ICAS, was attended by over 150 researchers and professionals from 12 countries. The event featured 35 paper presentations and a panel discussion on climate policy in the Hindu Kush Himalayan region.

fields.title: ICAS Launches Women in STEM Scholarship Programme
fields.content: With support from the US Embassy Kathmandu, ICAS has launched a scholarship and mentorship programme targeting female undergraduate students in engineering and applied sciences. Applications are now open for the 2024-25 cohort.
